name: core/component
description: Core classes and mechanisms that define how UI components are created and managed within the library

name: core/events
description: Internal implementation of global event handling.

name: core
description:
Public API for the core modules.

This file re-exports the internal implementations from the `core` directory
with new names where appropriate, defining the public-facing API for the
library bundle.

For internal library usage, import directly from the specific file within
the `core` directory (e.g., `import { ... } from './settings.js'`).

name: core/settings
description: Internal implementation for managing shared configuration.

name: ui/details-group
description: Manages groups of details (ie. onlyOneOpen at a time)

name: ui
description:
Public API for the UI modules.

This file re-exports the internal implementations from the `ui` directory
with new names where appropriate, defining the public-facing API for the
library bundle.

For internal library usage, import directly from the specific file within
the `ui` directory (e.g., `import { ... } from './dialog.js'`).

customTags.value:
How to Name Exports

Each export is renamed with a prefix based on its source module's name
(e.g., `init` from `dialog.js` becomes `dialogInit`). This is a critical
part of the library's bundling strategy, which creates a single, "flat"
module with uniquely named exports.

This approach:
1. Prevents name collisions between modules that might use the same export
   names (like `init` or `defaults`).
2. Allows bundlers in consuming projects to perform dead-code elimination
   (tree-shaking) with maximum effectiveness.

How To Add New Exports

When adding a new export, follow the naming convention:
`[moduleName][OriginalExportName]` (using camelCase).

For example, to export `myFunction` from `./foo.js`, you would add:
`myFunction as fooMyFunction`

Please keep the exports within each block alphabetized.

name: ui/modal-builder
description: Note this module needs to be initialized before dialogs!

name: ui/page
description:
General/Document Related 
- Add custom properties for scrollbar

name: ui/proxy-click
description:
Used for cards and things that look like they should be clickable 
even though the link in their content is the only clickable element. This way 
the entire cards content doesn't need to be in a link (which isn't accessible). 

The script allows only for clicks with a duration of 250ms to avoid conflict 
with a user selecting text. Works with either links or buttons because it just 
uses the elements .click(). Uses data-attributes for selection by default.

name: ui/scrollpoint
description: Module that uses intersection observer to add scrollpoint like behavior.

name: utils/dialog
description: Utility functions for dialogs

name: utils/font-awesome
description: Utility module for setting up Font Awesome

name: utils
description:
Public API for the utility modules.

This file re-exports the internal implementations from the `utils` directory
with new names where appropriate, defining the public-facing API for the
library bundle.

For internal library usage, import directly from the specific file within
the `utils` directory (e.g., `import { ... } from './dom.js'`).

customTags.value:
Naming Convention
To ensure tree-shaking works correctly while avoiding name collisions in the
final library, the following naming convention is used for re-exporting:

1. Generic Names: Functions with highly generic names (e.g., `log`, `set`, `defaults`)
   MUST be prefixed with a name derived from their module to prevent conflicts.
   Example: `export { log as classLoggerLog } from "./class-logger.js";`

2. Descriptive Names: Functions with specific, descriptive names that are unlikely
   to cause conflicts (e.g., `newId`, `createFloatingUi`, `configureIcons`)
   can be re-exported directly without a prefix for better readability.
   Example: `export { newId } from "./id.js";`

name: TabManager
description:
Class for managing  Aria tabs
- Designed to be minimal and lightweight but cover all traditional needs
- Designed for static / traditional webpages (not SPA)
- Separated from tabs.js so it can be used by itself as needed (tree-shaking)

name: FileSave
description:
Simple script that is useful for testing
- Make a file 
- Create a URL to it
- Gives utility function to create a link to the file (for front end)
- Gives method to destroy the file when no longer needed
- User can redefine the program by passing options object matching props.     
Limited to new browsers that support Blob(), also user preferences or type of browser may limit access to Blob functionality

This function returns an object with a `toString()` method that, when called,
retrieves the current value of the specified setting. This allows the setting
to be used as a string literal, dynamically retrieving its value.

Optional function to transform the setting's
value when its string representation is requested.

returns.description:
An object with a `toString()` method that returns the
(optionally transformed) setting value as a string.

name: createPager
description:
Function to be used in overflow scrollers "amount" option. This function will
determine how many items can fit in the viewport, taking into account scroll padding left, 
and will set the scroll amount to paginate between items. Items size can be anything 
(ie. one per screen vs 3.5 per screen will both work). This seperated from the plugin 
for tree shaking incase it's unneeded. Currently this is only setup for horizontal scrolling

Note: This is setup to return the function, incase configuration is needed in the future 
it can be passed to the create function

returns.description: A function to be used in overflow scrollers "amount" configuration property

returns.description: The fully formed CSS custom property name (e.g., "--prefix-propertyName").

name: observeDialogToggle
description:
Workaround for poor Safari support of the dialog 'toggle' event.
Watches for changes to the 'open' attribute and fires a callback.

params.description: The dialog element to observe
params.name: dialog

params.description: Function to call when the open state changes. Receives boolean indicating open state.
params.name: callback

returns.description: Object with a destroy method to disconnect the observer

name: dataAttributeToDatasetKey
description: Converts a data attribute name to its corresponding dataset property name.

params.description: The data attribute name (e.g., "data-ulu-dialog").
params.name: dataAttribute

returns.description: - The dataset property name (e.g., "uluDialog").

name: setPositionClasses
description:
Sets up the positional classes that would come from the equal
  height module. Needs to be rerun by user when layout changes
  or new instances are added to the screen
  - Used for gutter crops
  - Used for rule placement
  - **Devs** Remember that default classes should match sass defaults

params.description: The grid parent <data-grid="">
params.name: parent

params.description: Override the default equal heights classes
params.name: classes

name: resolveClasses
description: Resolves a class input (string or array) into a consistent array of class names.

params.description: The class input, which can be a string, an array of strings, or any other value.
params.name: input

examples:
resolveClassArray("fas fa-check  my-class"); // Returns ["fas", "fa-check", "my-class"]
resolveClassArray(["another-class", "yet-another-class"]); // Returns ["another-class", "yet-another-class"]
resolveClassArray("single-class"); // Returns ["single-class"]

returns.description: An array of class names. Returns an empty array for invalid or falsy input.

name: isBrowserSupported
description: Check for Compatibility (optional, implement on user side)

params.description: Elements (trigger, content, and optionally contentArrow)
params.name: elements

params.description: Configuration options for floatingUi
params.name: options

returns.description: floating cleanup function call when no longer needed

name: configureIcons
description: Sets icon settings to Font Awesome icons

name: newId
description: Create new uid

name: ensureId
description: Sets an ID if element doesn't have one vie newUid

params.description: Element to make sure has an id
params.name: element

name: getSoleIframeLayout
description:
Checks if an element's sole content is an iframe, and determines its layout type.
Useful for determining if layout fixes should be applied to containers.

params.description: The container to check
params.name: container

returns.description: Returns an object with iframe details, or null if not a sole iframe

name: pauseVideos
description:
Somewhat hacky way to pause the video
- https://www.digitalredpanther.com/blog/play-pause-stop-youtube-embed
- Actual JS API documentation (Didn't follow this for now) (https://developers.google.com/youtube/iframe_api_reference)

params.description: The DOM element to search for and pause videos within
params.name: context

params.description: The YouTube API command. Defaults to 'stopVideo' to preserve legacy behavior.
params.name: command

name: prepVideos
description:
Prep videos to be paused
- Add query parameters for js API
- Removes all other query parameters from iframe.src

name: BreakpointDirection
description: Used to handle a breakpoints direction's handler and state

name: Breakpoint
description: Single breakpoint management

name: selfManaged
description: The module won't attach the handlers (you need to do it yourself)

name: startOpen
description: This collapsible starts in open state

name: openClass
description: Open/active state class

name: debug
description: Output debug info

name: multiplier
description: Amount to increase size by (ie. pointer movement * multiplier)

name: overrideMaxDimensions
description: Remove max-width, max-height

name: fromX
description: Specifies the horizontal edge from which resizing occurs.`null` means no horizontal resizing.- Default null

name: fromY
description: Specifies the vertical edge from which resizing occurs.- `null` means no vertical resizing.- Default null

name: keyboardStep
description: The step in pixels for keyboard resizing with arrow keys.

name: keyboardDebounceTime
description: Debounce time in milliseconds for ending a keyboard resize.

name: manageEvents
description:
If true, the Resizer instance will automatically bind its own DOM event listeners
(pointerdown, keydown) to the control element. If `false`, the user is
responsible for calling `resizerInstance.onPointerdown(event)` and
`resizerInstance.onKeydown(event)` from their own listeners.
Default: true

name: manageAriaLabel
description:
If true, the Resizer instance will automatically manage the `aria-label`
attribute of the control element. If `false`, the user is responsible
for setting this attribute.
Default: false

name: enablePointerResizing
description:
If true, pointer events (mouse/touch) will enable resizing.
Default: true

name: enableKeyboardResizing
description:
If true, keyboard events (arrow keys) will enable resizing.
Default: true

name: root
description: Default observer root element

name: rootSelector
description: Use a selector to select the observer root element

name: debug
description: Log debug info to console

name: horizontal
description: Change scroll orientation to horizontal

name: marginStart
description: Margin for observer top or left (depending on orientation)

name: marginEnd
description: Margin for observer bottom or right (depending on orientation)

name: threshold
description: Threshold for observer

name: exit
description: The point can exited (else persists)

name: exitForward
description: The point can exit from the end

name: exitReverse
description: The point can exit from the start

name: setClasses
description: Set state classes

name: classPrefix
description: Prefix for classes

name: setAttribute
description: Set attribute for state (less verbose same info as classes)

name: attributeName
description: Attribute name to set state info in

name: syncElements
description: Elements that should also get active state info (classes or attributes)

name: accessible
description:
Should the tooltip and content be linked accessibly
- Note tooltips can only apply to interactive elements! (ie button, input, role="...", etc)

name: content
description: String/markup to insert into tooltip display

name: fromElement
description: Pull content from pre-existing content on page

name: fromAnchor
description: If used on a link that is an anchor link it will display the content of the anchor like fromElement

name: endOfDocument
description: Move the content to the bottom of the document

name: showEvents
description: Events to show tooltip on

name: hideEvents
description: Events to hide tooltip on

name: delay
description: Delay when using the directive

name: events
description: Event object - called on dispatch

name: defaults
description: Default options

name: config
description: Global Configuration Object

name: initResize
description: Setup resize handler/dispatch

name: initPrint
description: Setup print listeners

name: queryChildren
description: Queries all current children in element

name: setupChildren
description: Sets up any children not already setup in group

name: toggleHandler
description:
Toggle handler for child details element
- For things like one open at a time

name: destroy
description: Function removes all handlers and init attributes

name: prepVideos
description: Pause native and youtube videos for a given dialog

name: pauseVideos
description: Prep videos to be paused for a given dialog

name: separateDialogOptions
description: Returns JSON string to embed in data-ulu-dialog for dialog handling

params.description: Config object to pull dialog specific settings from
params.name: config

name: setupTrigger
description: Setup a single trigger (can be used manually without attr if needed)

name: setupSlider
description: Setup instance of scroll slider based on data-attributes

params.description: The scroll slider container
params.name: container

name: onChange
description: Callback called when state changes

name: toggleState
description: Instance function to get the next theme in cycle

name: onToggleClick
description: Handler for click for both toggle and remote toggles

name: attachRemotes
description:
Utility to attach remote handlers
- Used initially and when page is modified

name: cleanupRemotes
description:
This only cleans up remotes that are still in DOM
- For ones that have been removed we don't store any references to them

name: destroy
description: Function to cleanup listeners and remove init attributes

name: setState
description: Change the state of target/toggle

name: resolveInitial
description:
Function determines what the initial state is
- Check OS preference, saved preference, or initialState depending on options

returns.description: The resolved initial theme's key

name: getMatchingThemeQuery
description: Check each theme for a matching media query

returns.description: Matching theme key

name: getNextThemeKey
description: Get the next key in the themes based on the currentKey

name: getOtherThemes
description: Get all other theme object except the current

name: concatThemeClasses
description: Concatenates multiple class properties into one array

name: getStorageKey
description: Creates the storage key (either prefix or prefix with group name)

name: template
description: Template for the content display

name: onChange
description: Callback when tooltip is shown or hidden

name: Defaults
description: Default settings

name: DefaultModalOptions
description:
Default builder options (extends dialog defaults, watch name collisions)
- Decided to extend defaults so the interface in HTML is singular
- This is sometimes easier to template (merging and serializing options
in twig for example)

params.description: The ID for the new modal.
params.name: template.id

params.description: The resolved modal options.
params.name: template.config

returns.description: The HTML string for the close icon.
The HTML string for the resizer icon.
Markup for the modal.

properties.description: The title of the modal. Defaults to `null`.
properties.name: title

properties.description: The class name for an icon to display in the title. Defaults to `null`.
properties.name: titleIcon

properties.description: Extra class/classes to add to title
properties.name: titleClass

properties.description: Set the aria-labelledby attribute to a specific title within the modal, to connect to a custom title implementation, if using built in title this will be set automatically
properties.name: labelledby

properties.description: Set the aria-describedby on the dialog, elements id, to tie a specific part of the content to be the accessible description
properties.name: describedby

properties.description: If `true`, the modal will not prevent interaction with elements behind it. Defaults to `false`.
properties.name: nonModal

properties.description: If `true`, the modal will be appended to the end of the `document.body`. Defaults to `true`.
properties.name: documentEnd

properties.description: If `true`, the modal will be resizable. Defaults to `false`.
properties.name: allowResize

properties.description: The initial position of the modal. Defaults to `"center"`.
properties.name: position

properties.description: If `true`, the modal body will fill the available space. Defaults to `false`.
properties.name: bodyFills

properties.description: If `true`, no backdrop will be displayed behind the modal. Defaults to `false`.
properties.name: noBackdrop

properties.description: The size of the modal. Defaults to `"default"`.
properties.name: size

properties.description: If `true`, the modal content will be optimized for printing. Defaults to `false`.
properties.name: print

properties.description: If `true`, the modal will not have a minimum height. Defaults to `false`.
properties.name: noMinHeight

properties.description: Additional CSS class(es) to add to the modal. Defaults to `""`.
properties.name: class

properties.description: The base CSS class for the modal elements. Defaults to `"modal"`.
properties.name: baseClass

properties.description: The class name for the close button. Defaults to 'button button--icon' styling.
properties.name: classClose

properties.description: The class name for the close icon. Defaults to 'button__icon' styling. Uses the wrapped setting string.
properties.name: classCloseIcon

properties.description: The class name for the resizer icon. Uses the wrapped setting string.
properties.name: classResizerIcon

properties.description: Element or selector to use as the footer (will be moved to dialog on creation, used for DOM API)
properties.name: footerElement

properties.description: Markup to use in the footer
properties.name: footerHtml

properties.description: Enables debug logging. Defaults to `false`.
properties.name: debug

properties.description: Opt-in convenience behavior. If the modal body's sole content is an iframe, it automatically applies layout fixes. If the iframe has static width/height attributes (like YouTube), it retains that aspect ratio responsively. Otherwise, it forces the iframe to fill the modal. Defaults to `false`.
properties.name: autoIframe

properties.description: A function that returns the HTML for the close icon.
properties.name: templateCloseIcon

properties.description: The resolved modal configuration object.
properties.name: templateCloseIcon.config

properties.description: A function that returns the HTML for the resizer icon.
properties.name: templateResizerIcon

properties.description: The resolved modal configuration object.
properties.name: templateResizerIcon.config

properties.description: The default modal template function.
properties.name: template

properties.description: "horizontal"|"vertical", auto-detected if omitted.
properties.name: orientation

properties.description: Index to activate on load.
properties.name: initialIndex

properties.description: Allow all arrow keys to navigate regardless of orientation.
properties.name: allArrows

properties.description: Activate tab based on URL hash on initialization.
properties.name: openByUrlHash

properties.description: Update URL hash when a new tab is activated.
properties.name: setUrlHash

properties.description: Automatically match the height of all panels.
properties.name: equalHeights

properties.description: Callback fired after initialization: (instance) => {}
properties.name: onReady

properties.description: Callback fired when tab changes: (active, previous) => {}
